wp.token.antirug.p: $FLOW is positioned as a demand-side utility: every token in circulation was paid in by a client for real delivered work, and the protocol's own take is routed into the liquidity pool rather than a company wallet. The protocol therefore has no on-chain primitive that lets a team or admin unilaterally sell into the book — there is no treasury-initiated distribution, no admin mint, and no public retail sale. Rug-risk, as commonly understood, requires one of: (i) a team/insider vested wallet that unlocks and dumps, (ii) an admin-controlled mint function, or (iii) a one-sided LP withdrawal. The protocol explicitly foregoes all three: team and investor allocations are locked for a minimum of 12 months, mint authority is revoked at TGE, and LP contributions are one-way sinks that cannot be withdrawn by any single role.
wp.token.liquidity.p: Liquidity is fed from three independent, automatic channels: (a) locked TVL — every active job locks its full budget in escrow for the 14-day window, so the pool's floor scales with concurrent GMV; (b) 20% per delivered deal — on ACCEPTED the settlement contract routes 20% of the principal directly into the LP instead of a platform fee wallet; (c) 1% deposit drip — at JobRequest memo creation, 1% of the deposit is diverted to the LP in the same transaction, before execution begins. Worked example on a $100 order: day 0 → $1 to LP (c) + $100 locked (a); day 15 → $20 to LP (b), $15–20 to the agent-owner royalty wallet, remainder to the operator's vesting contract. Over a long horizon the LP grows approximately as 0.21 × cumulative settled GMV plus a spike-smoothed floor from active escrow.
wp.token.split.p: On acceptance the principal is divided three ways: 20% → liquidity sink, 15–20% → agent owner royalties, 60–65% → operator share. The operator share is inclusive of inference cost (Claude, GPT, open-source) — the operator picks the model mix per task and keeps the margin between the fixed client price and their compute bill. This deliberately replaces the older 1% flat platform fee: the protocol gives up a direct cash-flow and gains a mechanically-deepening pool. Operator earnings are not paid as a lump sum on day 0; the operator share enters the vesting schedule (2%/day for 14 days, then 72% on day 15) described in Chapter 02. The combined effect: clients get a fixed price, operators get long-tail reputation incentive, and the token is backed by a pool that only grows.
wp.glossary.anti_rug.p: A protocol whose design forecloses the common rug-pull surfaces: no admin mint, no one-sided LP withdrawal, no unlocked insider allocations at launch, and no treasury-initiated token distribution. Golden Connect's $FLOW meets these criteria by construction: supply is fixed at TGE, mint authority is revoked, LP sinks are one-way, and all team/investor allocations are locked for at least 12 months.
wp.glossary.liquidity_sink.p: A contract-level routing rule that sends a fixed share of protocol cashflow into the $FLOW liquidity pool with no reverse path. Golden Connect runs three such sinks: 20% of each accepted deal's principal, 1% of every new deposit, and the full budget of every active escrow (lock, not burn). Sinks grow monotonically with usage and are the token's primary float-support mechanism.
